member.name: Margaret Mullane
member.synopsis: Margaret Mullane is the Labour MP for Dagenham and Rainham , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Networks: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what powers Ofgem has to intervene where heat network prices are considered excessive.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the regulator of heat networks, Ofgem have broad powers relating to price regulation. These include powers to investigate and intervene where heat network prices charged to consumers are disproportionate or unfair.Under Ofgem’s authorisation regime, he...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Networks: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what protections are in place for consumers on heat networks wishing to challenge charges before the completion of the Ofgem authorisation process in January 2027.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the introduction of the heat network regulator, Ofgem rules require operators and suppliers to charge consumers fairly and guidance has been published on the application of their fair pricing rules. Heat networks have until January 2027 to register ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Networks: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what his Department's planned timetable is for the full implementation of the heat network regulatory framework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government has introduced statutory redress through the energy ombudsman and statutory advice and advocacy through Citizens Advice and Consumer Scotland for heat network consumers from April 2025. The Department has subsequently introduced Ofgem as the he...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the value-for-money to the taxpayer of the retrospective Loan Charge.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answers I gave on 9 February 2026 to UINs 109841, 109843 and 109842.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how many outstanding cases of people facing the retrospective Loan Charge she expects will be settled as a result of the McCann Review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answers I gave on 9 February 2026 to UINs 109841, 109843 and 109842.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Warm Homes Plan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to increase the jobs and skills needed in the construction and retrofit sectors to meet the delivery targets in the Warm Homes Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Warm Homes Plan will support up to 180,000 additional jobs in energy efficiency and clean heating by 2030. It includes our £8 million Warm Homes Skills Programme and a £7 million Heat Training Grant already helping people gain requisite skills and qua...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what plans he has to future proof existing and new housing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Future Homes and Buildings Standards will come into force on 24 March 2027 and there will be a further transitional period for one year afterwards to allow the construction sector to transition smoothly to the new standards. The FHS reduces energy dem...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Development: Religion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what discussions his department has had with faith groups and local authorities promoting faith covenants since the Prime Minister’s commitment in June 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government recognises the value of partnership working at the local level to support communities, including between faith and belief groups and local authorities. Faith Covenants are an important example of such partnerships. The Civil Society Covena...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what discussions his Department has had with internet search providers on preventing unofficial websites from presenting themselves as government service portals and charging inflated f
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Digital Service works with a number of external partners, including internet search providers, to help prevent unofficial websites from presenting themselves as government services and charging inflated fees. This includes identifying and r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent steps she has taken to help improve public awareness of the risks associated with using unofficial third party websites for accessing government services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department continues to strengthen GOV.UK as the authoritative source of online government information and services.This includes upholding its clear and consistent identity so that users recognise and trust official content, supporting efforts to cou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the prevalence of third party websites charging for government services that are otherwise free or low cost.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While the department has not made a formal assessment of the prevalence of third‑party websites that charge for government services which are otherwise free or low cost, it is reducing their impact through prevention, enhancing awareness and collaborating...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit: Cost of Living
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department has made of the potential merits of commissioning independent advice on the cost of essentials when setting the rate of the Universal Credit Standard Allowance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has taken important steps to improve the support available to help people with the cost of essentials. The Universal Credit Act will deliver the first sustained above inflation rise in the standard allowance of Universal Credit since it was...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Cost of Living
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if his Department will make an assessment of the potential merits of introducing an independent process to set benefit levels in line with essential costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Social Security Administration Act 1992 requires the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions to review benefit and State Pension rates each year to see if they have retained their value in relation to the general level of prices or earnings. Where th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing Associations: Economic Crime Levy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of increases to the Economic Crime Levy on not-for-profit housing associations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government published its summary of the impacts of the increases to the Economic Crime (Anti-Money Laundering) Levy in the policy paper titled "Economic Crime Levy – changes to bands and charges” (Economic Crime Levy — changes to bands and charge...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing: Regeneration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to support the regeneration of existing social housing stock.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the Written Ministerial Statement made on 28 January 2026 (HCWS1283).
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what progress his Department has made on ensuring infrastructure accompanies new housing delivery.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Planning Policy Framework sets out that the purpose of the planning system is to contribute to the achievement of sustainable development, including the provision of supporting infrastructure in a sustainable manner. It also supports the incr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help ensure social housing delivery reduces the number of families waiting for (a) three, (b) four, and (c) 5 bedroom homes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Planning Policy Framework (NPPF) makes clear that local authorities should assess the size, type, and tenure of housing needed for different groups, including those who require affordable and social rent homes, and should reflect this in thei...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Temporary Accommodation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help reduce local authority reliance on temporary accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our National Plan to End Homelessness the government is putting prevention at the heart of public services, alongside with actions to address the root causes of homelessness through building more homes, reforming renters’ rights, and tackling pove...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Supported Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to deliver a sustainable supported housing sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government values the vital role played by supported housing in helping older, disabled and other vulnerable people to live independently and well, and the contribution it makes to wider government objectives, including tackling rough sleeping and hom...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to ensure that the hardest to reach young people are supported by the National Youth Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Youth Strategy outlines our plan to increase young people’s access to enriching activities, safe spaces and support from trusted adults. A key ambition of the Strategy is to halve the participation gap in enriching activities between disadvan...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport
